customer_addr:
195 Prairie Lake Rd
East Dundee, IL 60118

citations.narrative:
The facility’s written contingency plan describes the possibility of needing to evacuate animals in situations such as a fire,
tornado or other hazard in the building. However, the plan does not provide detailed evacuation instructions. Having a
plan in place will help provide for the humane handling, treatment, transportation, housing, and care of their animals in the
event an emergency or disaster occurs. Correct by ensuring the written contingency plan includes all required information.
Correct by 07/01/2026.

Per a facility representative, employees have not received formal training on the contingency plan. Lack of training for
personnel that have responsibilities during emergencies may lead to a lack of coordination and preventable losses. The
facility must provide training for its personnel regarding their roles and responsibilities as outlined in the plan. Correct by
07/01/2026.

citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
At the time of inspection, the following animals were found with medical conditions the facility had not identified during the
morning daily observations:
- A djungarian hamster maintained his right eye closed. The area all along the eye was hairless and the exposed skin was
red and bruised. He also had an approximately half an inch long hairless region at the top of his head, also with red and
bruised skin exposed.
- A brown, tan and white guinea pig was found with an approximately 1 inch long and 0.5 inch wide hairless area over his
lower back. The bottom section of the skin in that hairless region was red while the top was covered in crusty white and
gray material.
- A tan and brown guinea pig with a white nose was seen with crusty yellow and brown material coating the area from his
nostrils to his top lip. The skin over the nostrils was reddened.
- A black and white roborovski hamster was found with both eyes shut. The hamster did eventually open the eyes,
however the area around both was hairless and reddened.
- A white Syrian hamster was found with his left eye closed at first. He eventually opened the eye however the hair all
around was wet and coated in a crusty white material.
Additionally, the facility did not identify two dead roborovski hamsters housed within the same enclosure and cold to the
touch when found.
Daily observations are not being done sufficiently and thus did not identify the condition affecting these animals. The
licensee must ensure that daily observation of all animals occur in order to assess their health and well-being. Timely
communication with the attending veterinarian must occur to provide proper diagnosis and medical attention. Correct by
04/29/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

2.38(l)(1)(ii) 
   
Miscellaneous. 
The facility’s written contingency plan describes the possibility of needing to evacuate animals in situations such as a fire, 
tornado or other hazard in the building. However, the plan does not provide detailed evacuation instructions. Having a 
plan in place will help provide for the humane handling, treatment, transportation, housing, and care of their animals in the 
event an emergency or disaster occurs. Correct by ensuring the written contingency plan includes all required information. 
Correct by 07/01/2026. 
2.38(l)(3) 
   
Miscellaneous. 
Per a facility representative, employees have not received formal training on the contingency plan. Lack of training for 
personnel that have responsibilities during emergencies may lead to a lack of coordination and preventable losses. The 
facility must provide training for its personnel regarding their roles and responsibilities as outlined in the plan. Correct by 
07/01/2026. 
2.40(b)(3) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
At the time of inspection, the following animals were found with medical conditions the facility had not identified during the 
morning daily observations:  
- A djungarian hamster maintained his right eye closed. The area all along the eye was hairless and the exposed skin was 
red and bruised. He also had an approximately half an inch long hairless region at the top of his head, also with red and 
bruised skin exposed. 
- A brown, tan and white guinea pig was found with an approximately 1 inch long and 0.5 inch wide hairless area over his 
lower back. The bottom section of the skin in that hairless region was red while the top was covered in crusty white and 
gray material. 
- A tan and brown guinea pig with a white nose was seen with crusty yellow and brown material coating the area from his 
nostrils to his top lip. The skin over the nostrils was reddened. 
- A black and white roborovski hamster was found with both eyes shut. The hamster did eventually open the eyes, 
however the area around both was hairless and reddened. 
- A white Syrian hamster was found with his left eye closed at first. He eventually opened the eye however the hair all

around was wet and coated in a crusty white material. 
Additionally, the facility did not identify two dead roborovski hamsters housed within the same enclosure and cold to the 
touch when found.  
Daily observations are not being done sufficiently and thus did not identify the condition affecting these animals. The 
licensee must ensure that daily observation of all animals occur in order to assess their health and well-being. Timely 
communication with the attending veterinarian must occur to provide proper diagnosis and medical attention. Correct by 
04/29/2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
